name: Hearth and Horizon Next Kiln Packs
rules: Keep generated GLBs in public/assets/kiln/generated until intake proof passes.
Do not add these entries to tools/kiln/assets-catalog.json until the generation run is approved.
Promote only after proof:kiln-assets and proof:kiln-asset-viewer pass.
Use generated GLBs as skins, bodies, props, or dressing; terrain, water, mining cracks, route ribbons, glows, particles, and collision truth stay code-owned.

packs.reason: Door, window, and roof GLBs are approved inserts, but the game still needs actual wall pieces that share one dimensional contract.
requiresBeforeCatalog: Code-owned wall socket dimensions
Hex-edge pivot and snap rule
Wall, opening, roof, and foundation enclosure contribution rules

manifest.name: Sphere Planet Shared-Scale House Shell
manifest.sourceIntent: A coherent modular low-poly house shell kit for a hex-tile spherical survival game. Every member must share the same wall height, timber thickness, pivot convention, opening scale, palette, and weathered plank language so door, window, wall, rail, roof, and foundation pieces can snap together visually.

items.name: wall-panel
items.prompt: A single modular low-poly wooden wall panel for a survival hut: flat plank face, two side posts, top and bottom rails, same height and thickness as all other wall pieces, no door, no window, no floor, no roof.

items.name: wall-window-opening
items.prompt: A modular wooden wall panel with a centered window opening: same outer size as the plain wall panel, timber posts and rails, a simple fitted frame around an empty window hole, no glass pane floating outside the wall.

items.name: wall-door-opening
items.prompt: A modular wooden wall panel with a centered door opening: same outer size as the plain wall panel, sturdy jamb posts and lintel, low threshold, no separate room, no roof, no loose freestanding doorway.

items.name: wall-corner-join
items.prompt: A compact low-poly corner join for two wooden wall panels meeting at a hex-friendly angle: shared-height vertical post, short angled rails, visible join caps, made to visually bridge two wall faces.

items.name: wall-half-rail
items.prompt: A half-height modular wooden rail wall: same width and timber language as the wall panel, two low posts, top rail, lower planks, open upper air, useful for porches and garden edges.

items.name: floor-foundation
items.prompt: A single low modular wooden floor foundation panel for a hex survival hut: weathered plank deck, short underside beams, flat top, clean edge lip, no walls or roof.

items.name: roof-edge
items.prompt: A modular low-poly roof edge piece with weathered planks and a visible eave: same timber palette as the wall kit, made to cap one wall span, no full house, no walls.

items.name: roof-corner-cap
items.prompt: A compact modular roof corner cap for a small hex-friendly hut: angled plank planes meeting cleanly, short eave lips, same material language as roof-edge, no full building.

items.name: roof-support-post
items.prompt: A short sturdy timber roof support post with simple bracket arms and pegged caps, same height language as the wall kit, designed to sit under roof edges or porch rails.

packs.goalNode: K9 aquatic life and fishing visibility
packs.reason: Fishing already has systems, traps, nets, and fish-school rules, but there are no authored single fish bodies in the approved pack. Schools/flocks must be runtime instancing, not generated mini-scenes.
requiresBeforeCatalog: Fish-school visual owner in fishing or native-life renderer
Distance-gated swim animation policy
Waterline placement and culling proof
packs.runtimeOwner: Fishing sim + NativeLifeRenderer or dedicated FishSchoolRenderer

manifest.name: Sphere Planet Aquatic Single Bodies
manifest.sourceIntent: A coherent set of silly low-poly single aquatic character bodies for a spherical hex survival game. Generate exactly one creature or fish per item: no schools, no clusters, no foliage, no water base, no rocks, no props, and no mini-scenes. Runtime code will instance, scale, tint, and flock these bodies for fish schools.

items.name: fish-shore-minnow
items.prompt: Exactly one goofy low-poly shore fish character body for instancing: rounded single fish, tiny fins, big simple eyes, one or two color accents, centered at origin, no other fish, no school, no plants, no rocks, no water, no base, no scene.

items.name: fish-storm-runner
items.prompt: Exactly one low-poly storm-run fish character body for instancing: angular fast-looking single fish, bright fins, tiny glow accents on the body, centered at origin, no other fish, no school, no water, no weather effects, no base, no scene.

items.name: fish-cave-shimmer
items.prompt: Exactly one low-poly cave shimmer fish character body for instancing: rounded dark single fish, soft glowing belly, small fins, big curious eyes, centered at origin, no other fish, no school, no cave rocks, no water, no base, no scene.

items.name: creature-driftjelly
items.prompt: Exactly one gentle goofy open-ocean driftjelly creature character body: soft low-poly bell, dangling ribbon tendrils attached to the body, little face, glowing underside, centered at origin, no other creatures, no water, no plants, no base, no scene.

items.name: fish-reed-fry
items.prompt: Exactly one tiny reed-water fry fish character body for instancing: small rounded single bait fish, simple eyes, short fins, compact readable silhouette, centered at origin, no other fish, no reed scrap, no plants, no water, no base, no scene.

packs.goalNode: K11 sky life and bird boids
packs.reason: Birds should be authored as single animated bodies. Runtime code will instance, scale, flock, perch, and distance-gate them instead of baking flocks or scenery into GLBs.
requiresBeforeCatalog: Bird visual owner and boids budget
Distance-gated flap animation policy
Per-biome sky-life spawn rules
packs.runtimeOwner: Future SkyLifeRenderer + KilnRuntimeAssets bird body provider

manifest.name: Sphere Planet Sky Life Single Bodies
manifest.sourceIntent: A coherent set of silly low-poly single bird character bodies for a spherical hex survival game. Generate exactly one bird per item: no flocks, no second bird, no branch, no tree, no nest, no sky background, no clouds, no rocks, no water, no base, and no mini-scenes. Runtime code will instance, scale, tint, perch, and flock these bodies.

items.name: bird-sky-kite
items.prompt: Exactly one goofy low-poly sky-kite bird character body for instancing: wide simple wings, small rounded body, tiny tail fan, big simple eyes, centered at origin in a neutral flying pose, no other birds, no flock, no branch, no tree, no sky, no base, no scene.

items.name: bird-shore-gull
items.prompt: Exactly one silly low-poly shore gull bird character body for instancing: compact white-grey body, long simple beak, wide wings, tiny feet tucked close, readable single-bird silhouette, centered at origin, no other birds, no flock, no water, no dock, no rocks, no base, no scene.

items.name: bird-forest-flutter
items.prompt: Exactly one goofy low-poly forest flutter bird character body for instancing: round colorful body, short wings, small tail feathers, oversized curious eyes, centered at origin in a neutral flying pose, no other birds, no flock, no branch, no leaves, no tree, no base, no scene.

items.name: bird-storm-finch
items.prompt: Exactly one low-poly storm finch bird character body for instancing: angular small body, swept wings, tiny crest, bright accent tips, big simple eyes, centered at origin in a neutral flying pose, no lightning effect, no other birds, no flock, no clouds, no weather scene, no base.

packs.reason: Wood and ore drops are GLB-backed now; common future resources still need authored pickup bodies.
requiresBeforeCatalog: Item taxonomy locked enough to map slug to item id
Ground-drop instancing path extended beyond wood and ore

manifest.name: Sphere Planet Pickup Drop Skins
manifest.sourceIntent: A coherent pickup-drop prop set for small collectible items that arc and bounce on the ground after chopping, mining, fishing, farming, or creature interactions.

items.name: drop-dirt-clod
items.prompt: A small low-poly dirt clod pickup: lumpy brown soil chunk with a few tiny root hairs, compact and readable on a hex tile.

items.name: drop-sand-pile
items.prompt: A small low-poly sand pile pickup: soft faceted mound with two or three pale shell flecks, compact and readable.

items.name: drop-snow-clump
items.prompt: A small low-poly snow clump pickup: rounded white chunk with a faint blue shadow side, compact and readable.

items.name: drop-glow-crystal
items.prompt: A small low-poly glowing crystal pickup: two or three faceted shards on a dark pebble base, bright but not huge.

items.name: drop-raw-fish
items.prompt: A small low-poly raw fish pickup: one compact fish body with simple fins and eye, slightly curved, readable as food on the ground.

items.name: drop-kelp-reeds
items.prompt: A small low-poly bundle of kelp and reeds tied with a cord, green-brown strands, compact collectible pickup.

items.name: drop-compost-pellet
items.prompt: A small low-poly compost pellet pickup: dark crumbly ball with tiny leaf bits, compact and readable as farming material.

items.name: drop-cave-mushroom
items.prompt: A small low-poly cave mushroom pickup: two squat glowing caps and a stubby stem cluster, compact collectible size.

items.name: drop-creature-fiber
items.prompt: A small low-poly tuft of soft creature fiber: rounded woolly puff clump with a tied thread, compact pickup size.

packs.reason: The first 12 domain nodes are wired, but survival progression needs clearer ore, clay, geode, and glow-crystal extraction targets.
requiresBeforeCatalog: Future ore/resource item ids named
Harvest yield and tool-tier rules drafted
Domain node placement weights updated

manifest.name: Sphere Planet Ore and Material Nodes
manifest.sourceIntent: A coherent resource-node set for visible harvest targets on a hex sphere. Each node should be a readable surface prop or embedded mound, not a terrain block or cave wall mesh.

items.name: node-iron-vein
items.prompt: A low-poly iron vein node: dark stone base with rusty metal streaks and a few angular exposed ore plates, compact enough for one hex.

items.name: node-copper-patina
items.prompt: A low-poly copper patina node: stone mound with green-blue oxidized copper flakes and warm copper edges, compact and readable.

items.name: node-coal-seam
items.prompt: A low-poly coal seam node: black angular chunks set into grey stone, a few dull highlights, compact one-hex harvest prop.

items.name: node-glow-crystal-vein
items.prompt: A low-poly glow-crystal vein node: dark rock base with several faceted glowing crystals growing from one side, readable in caves.

items.name: node-clay-bank
items.prompt: A low-poly clay bank node: damp reddish clay mound with a few layered cut faces and pebbles, meant for shore or cave-edge harvesting.

items.name: node-geode-pocket
items.prompt: A low-poly geode pocket node: cracked stone shell exposing small colorful crystal points inside, compact and readable as a rare find.

packs.goalNode: G5/K6R native roaming and ecology state
packs.reason: The nine approved creatures are wired as skins; roaming AI and ecology will benefit from a few more authored native families once the state machine exists.
requiresBeforeCatalog: NativeCreatureActor state machine
Clip hint contract for idle, walk, flee, warn, and interact
Distance-band animation budget proof

manifest.name: Sphere Planet Native Life Expansion
manifest.sourceIntent: A coherent creature expansion using the goofy low-poly Soft-Facet native-life language. These are planet-native characters with readable silhouettes, not generic enemies.

items.name: creature-pocket-puff
items.prompt: A tiny companion moss-puff creature: round fuzzy low-poly body, little feet, big curious eyes, leaf sprout on top, friendly and goofy, small enough to follow the player.

items.name: creature-frostpuff
items.prompt: A friendly cold-biome puff creature: woolly round body, icy blue accents, tiny feet, warm sleepy eyes, a little snow cap, goofy and harmless.

items.name: creature-skylift
items.prompt: A goofy balloon-bird creature for grasslands: round floating body, tiny dangling legs, little wing fins, simple beak, big eyes, readable as gentle flying life.

items.name: creature-glowmoth
items.prompt: A safe cave glowmoth creature: soft low-poly moth body, oversized glowing wings, tiny antennae, calm face, readable as a cave-air marker.

items.name: creature-glidewing
items.prompt: A forest canopy glidewing creature: small low-poly body, wide leaf-like gliding wings, little feet, big watchful eyes, curious but skittish.

items.name: creature-domain-warden
items.prompt: A rare territorial domain warden: goofy but imposing low-poly native creature with faceted shell armor, crest fins, sturdy feet, bright warning markings, not humanoid.

packs.goalNode: K7 landmarks, wonders, and cave identity
packs.reason: The planet needs more authored wonder dressing once the current shrines and carved caves have verbs and placement proof.
requiresBeforeCatalog: Wonder placement rules by domain
Cave-dressing sockets that do not fake cave mouths
Readability review against ambiguous crystal-gate shapes
packs.runtimeOwner: Landmark, threshold-space, cave, and route renderers

manifest.name: Sphere Planet Wonder and Cave Dressing
manifest.sourceIntent: A coherent set of authored props and dressing for caves, arches, and pentagon-domain wonders. These decorate real carved spaces and landmarks; they are not replacement terrain or fake cave-mouth doors.

items.name: tide-underpass-ribs
items.prompt: A low-poly set of curved tide-smoothed stone ribs for the inside of a sea underpass, compact modular cave dressing, no doorway frame.

items.name: lantern-skylight-ring
items.prompt: A compact low-poly stone-and-wood skylight ring with tiny hanging lantern hooks, made to dress a cave ceiling opening, no full building.

items.name: root-vault-room-piece
items.prompt: A low-poly root-vault interior dressing piece: twisting roots around a small stone shelf and soil lip, for an underground room wall, not a complete cave.

items.name: horizon-gate-dressing
items.prompt: A low-poly horizon gate dressing prop: two low stone vanes and a small wind marker aligned like a route device, clear base, not a tilted crystal portal.

items.name: dripstone-cluster
items.prompt: A compact low-poly cave dripstone cluster with a few stalagmites and matching short stalactite tips, designed as dressing in a real cave space.

items.name: cave-mushroom-clump
items.prompt: A low-poly cave mushroom clump with squat caps and soft glow dots, compact enough for repeated cave floor dressing.

items.name: spring-seep-stones
items.prompt: A small low-poly spring seep prop: damp stones, a tiny water trickle groove, a few moss patches, for cave or hillside water hints.

items.name: sea-cave-tide-pool-stones
items.prompt: A compact low-poly tide-pool stone cluster with shells and wet dark edges, for dressing real sea-cave floors near water.

packs.reason: The procedural player is a contract placeholder. The authored player model, equipment, and tool props should come from the same approved asset path.
requiresBeforeCatalog: Socket map for hands, back, belt, head, and carried bundle
Animation list locked for survival verbs
Decision on Kiln-generated model versus dedicated character rig pass

manifest.name: Sphere Planet Wayfarer and Equipment
manifest.sourceIntent: A coherent low-poly silly-but-polished survivor character and equipment kit for a spherical hex survival game. The character should be charming and readable at normal play distance, not a capsule with boxes.

items.name: player-soft-facet-wayfarer
items.prompt: A silly polished low-poly survival wayfarer character: squat sturdy body, rounded faceted head, expressive simple face, little boots, short gloves, backpack straps, charming proportions, no generic capsule body.

items.name: equip-pack-frame
items.prompt: A compact low-poly backpack frame prop with bedroll straps, little pouches, and a clipped tool loop, made to mount on a character back socket.

items.name: equip-storm-cloak
items.prompt: A short low-poly storm cloak prop with chunky folds and a simple clasp, made to drape over a small survivor character without hiding the silhouette.

items.name: tool-hatchet
items.prompt: A low-poly survival hatchet prop: short wooden handle, chunky stone or iron blade, readable at hand-held size.

items.name: tool-pickaxe
items.prompt: A low-poly pickaxe prop with a short wood handle and faceted metal head, readable at hand-held size.

items.name: tool-fishing-rod
items.prompt: A low-poly fishing rod prop with a bent reed pole, simple line loop, tiny cork float, and wrapped grip, readable in a character hand.

items.name: tool-short-sword
items.prompt: A simple low-poly short survival blade prop: compact metal blade, wrapped handle, not fantasy ornate, readable for warding small creatures.

items.name: tool-bow
items.prompt: A compact low-poly reed-and-wood bow prop with a small string and bundled arrow notch, readable at hand-held size.

items.name: equip-folded-chart
items.prompt: A small low-poly folded map chart prop with tied corners and a route mark on top, made to hold in two hands or strap to a pack.
